name: workflow_orchestration
description: Coordinates sequential and parallel task execution across multiple agents or systems, managing dependencies, branching logic, and state propagation throughout the workflow lifecycle.
steps: Parse and validate workflow definition against schema
Build directed acyclic graph (DAG) of task dependencies
Identify parallelizable task branches
Initialize execution state and context propagation mechanism
Execute tasks respecting dependency constraints
Handle failures with retry policies and rollback procedures
Aggregate results and propagate state to dependent tasks
Return execution trace and final state
constraints: Must detect circular dependencies before execution
Task timeout must be configurable (default 30min)
Failed tasks block dependent tasks unless retry policy is set
State propagation must be atomic per task completion
Maximum 1000 concurrent tasks per workflow instance

name: trigger_condition_monitoring
description: Monitors external events, data state changes, and temporal triggers to determine when automated workflows should be initiated or adjusted, implementing event-driven architecture patterns.
steps: Register trigger definitions and configure monitoring intervals
Poll or subscribe to event sources based on trigger type
Evaluate condition expressions against current state/events
Apply cooldown logic to prevent trigger spam
Construct context-enriched trigger payload
Execute target workflow with trigger data if condition matches
Log trigger evaluation and execution results
Schedule next monitoring cycle
constraints: Event evaluation must complete within 5 seconds
Minimum cooldown is 1 second, maximum is 24 hours
Complex condition expressions limited to 3 nested comparisons
Maximum 500 active triggers per monitoring session
Trigger payload must not exceed 10MB
Schedule-based triggers support cron syntax only

name: error_recovery_orchestration
description: Implements intelligent error detection, classification, and automated remediation strategies including retries, fallback mechanisms, compensating transactions, and graceful degradation.
steps: Capture and normalize error information
Classify error into category (transient, permanent, resource, auth, logic)
Match error classification against recovery policies
Select optimal recovery strategy based on error type and context
Implement exponential backoff or linear retry based on policy
Execute fallback action if primary recovery fails
Log recovery attempts with timing and outcomes
Escalate to human operator if automated recovery exhausted
constraints: Retry maximum 5 attempts unless policy specifies otherwise
Exponential backoff multiplier must be between 1.5-2.0
Compensating transactions must be idempotent
Recovery execution timeout is 60 seconds
Escalation notifications must include full error context
Recovery policies cannot create infinite retry loops

name: data_pipeline_automation
description: Orchestrates ETL/ELT processes including data extraction, transformation, validation, enrichment, and loading across heterogeneous sources with schema mapping and quality enforcement.
steps: Validate pipeline definition and data source connectivity
Extract data from source systems with incremental/full options
Apply schema mapping and type coercion rules
Execute transformation rules in dependency order
Run data quality and consistency checks
Enrich data with contextual information from reference tables
Load data into target systems with transaction handling
Generate validation report and quality metrics
constraints: Maximum batch size per stage is 1 million records
Schema mapping must be bidirectional and reversible
Data quality checks must not exceed 10% of processing time
Transformation operations must be deterministic
Failed records must be logged with root cause analysis
Pipeline must support automatic resume from last successful stage

name: resource_allocation_scheduler
description: Manages dynamic allocation of computational resources, memory, and worker processes across competing tasks, optimizing for throughput, latency, and cost based on demand patterns.
steps: Collect current resource availability and utilization metrics
Prioritize pending tasks by priority level and deadline
Calculate resource requirements for each task
Apply scheduling algorithm to match tasks to available resources
Account for task dependencies and inter-process communication overhead
Reserve resources with preemption policies for critical tasks
Assign tasks to specific worker instances
Monitor and adjust allocations as tasks complete or new tasks arrive
constraints: Scheduling algorithm must complete within 2 seconds
No task can be starved longer than its deadline period
Resource fragmentation must not exceed 20%
Preemption allowed only for tasks marked as preemptible
Critical priority tasks guaranteed minimum 50% of free resources
Memory allocation must include 10% buffer for system overhead

name: state_synchronization
description: Maintains consistency across distributed state replicas, handles conflicts, and ensures eventual consistency using conflict resolution strategies and reconciliation protocols.
steps: Collect state snapshots from all active replicas
Compare states to identify divergences
Detect conflicts where replicas have different values for same fields
Apply conflict resolution strategy based on configuration
Compute canonical state that represents all replicas
Distribute canonical state to all replicas
Verify replicas received and applied state updates
Log synchronization result with conflict resolution trace
constraints: Synchronization must complete within 30 seconds
Vector clock depth must not exceed 1000 nodes
Custom merge functions must be pure and deterministic
Conflict detection must identify all divergent fields
Eventual consistency timeout maximum 5 minutes
State snapshots must be cryptographically signed for integrity

name: task_dependency_resolver
description: Analyzes task interdependencies, detects blocking conditions, computes optimal execution order, and manages transitive dependencies to enable maximum parallelization.
steps: Parse task definitions and dependency declarations
Build directed acyclic graph representing dependencies
Detect circular dependencies and report as errors
Compute transitive closure to identify all indirect dependencies
Identify critical path using topological sort and duration analysis
Group tasks into parallelizable levels by dependency distance
Analyze optional dependencies and soft constraints
Generate optimized execution plan maximizing parallelism
constraints: Cycle detection must be O(V+E) complexity
Maximum 10,000 tasks per resolution
Optional dependencies cannot create new critical paths
Temporal constraints must be relative (not absolute times)
Execution plan must be deterministic given same inputs
Constraint validation must complete within 5 seconds

name: rate_limiting_enforcement
description: Enforces rate limits, quota policies, and throttling rules across distributed systems using token bucket, sliding window, and adaptive algorithms to prevent resource exhaustion.
steps: Load rate limit policy for requested resource
Determine applicable algorithm (token bucket, sliding window, or adaptive)
Calculate tokens/quota available based on time elapsed
Check if current request can be served within limits
Apply burst allowance if permitted and available
Record request in rate limit history
Calculate backoff time if rate limit exceeded
Adjust limits adaptively based on load patterns if enabled
constraints: Token bucket refill must be continuous, not batch
Window size minimum 1 second, maximum 1 hour
Burst allowance cannot exceed 200% of base limit
Adaptive adjustment factor must be between 0.5-2.0
Hard enforcement blocks excess requests; soft allows with penalty
Rate limit decisions must complete in under 10ms
